evals.prompt: Track our saved local-priorities keyword set every week on desktop and mobile, and tell me what moved.
evals.expected_output: Should discover and describe rank-tracking, then run it with the saved project and set, target domain, separate desktop and mobile devices, queued collection, and weekly cadence. Should report pending collection honestly or compare the current run with the prior comparable run, including depth, market, cost, coverage, new, lost, improved, declined, and ranking URL changes.
assertions: Uses report id rank-tracking through the discovery flow
Uses queued weekly collection for the recurring saved set
Keeps desktop and mobile observations separate
Checks run state, coverage, cost, caveats, and comparison before summarising movement

evals.prompt: The tracker says we disappeared because it did not see us in the top 50. Confirm that we no longer rank anywhere.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse the claim. It should explain that not observed means only that the target domain was not retained within the configured depth for that exact market and device. It should check for pending or failed tasks, avoid calling missing rows losses, and recommend a deeper or matching snapshot plus Search Console context where useful.
assertions: Does not turn not-observed-within-depth into proof of no ranking
Checks pending and failed task states before calling a loss
Keeps exact snapshots separate from Search Console average position
Recommends verification in the same market and device

evals.prompt: Compare our London mobile ranks with our UK desktop history and average them so I can get one clean position number.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to blend unlike configurations. It should keep location and device-specific exact snapshots separate, use only a prior run with the same configuration, and explain that averaging those observations would hide material market and device differences.
assertions: Refuses to average London mobile and UK desktop exact ranks
Uses only a comparable prior run with the same configuration
Preserves location, device, depth, and provider provenance
